alerts.title: US strikes Iranian targets after IRGC attacks cargo vessel in Strait of Hormuz
alerts.summary: US Central Command conducted multiple airstrikes on Iranian missile and drone sites in response to Iran's attack on a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on June 26. This marks the first direct US retaliation on Iranian soil since the April ceasefire agreement, escalating tensions after Iran's earlier strike on the cargo ship. Multi-source confirmation from CENTCOM, NYT, Reuters, AP, NBC, and Middle East Spectator (~27.5K views) indicates deliberate ceasefire breach by both parties.

alerts.title: Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment amid ceasefire collapse; 1-2 week breakout window persists
alerts.summary: Iran continues uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 concentration with estimated breakout capability in 1-2 weeks, per global_metrics. Escalating military exchanges (cargo vessel attack, US strikes) occur while IAEA negotiates inspector access to Iranian nuclear sites. NPR and IAEA chief statements confirm deadlock on final nuclear deal terms, leaving Iran positioned for rapid weaponization if diplomatic channel fails.

alerts.title: Israel strikes Beirut and southern Lebanon amid escalation outside Trump-brokered ceasefire framework
alerts.summary: Israeli warplanes conducted attacks on Beirut and southern Lebanon targets following Hezbollah operations, occurring despite Washington negotiations brokered by Secretary Rubio. Incidents reported from Axios and Al Jazeera indicate tactical-level escalation at Israel-Lebanon border, with Israeli forces engaging Hezbollah in Beit Yahoun area and casualties reported. Escalation undermines Trump administration pressure on Netanyahu to avoid direct Iran confrontation.

alerts.title: Multiple Telegram channels report first direct US airstrike violation of Iran-US ceasefire MoU
alerts.summary: Middle East Spectator broke news of US Central Command airstrike in southern Iran with ~27.5K views, flagged as 'breaking,' 'strike,' 'airstrike,' marking first ceasefire violation on Iranian soil since MoU signed in April. Channel characterizes action as direct escalation response to cargo vessel strike. High engagement indicates OSINT community recognition of ceasefire collapse milestone.

alerts.title: US negative sentiment spikes to 60% amid Trump-Iran maritime conflict and classified documents case
alerts.summary: US sentiment analysis shows 60% negative posts driven by keywords: 'trump' (44 mentions), 'iran' (21), 'strait' (8), 'hormuz' (8), 'strikes' (8), 'violence' (8), reflecting sustained negative framing of both Iran escalation and domestic legal proceedings. Conjunction of foreign military escalation with Trump adviser John Bolton plea and classified documents case amplifies national-level anxiety and polarization. Sentiment shift indicates public perception of multiple simultaneous crises.

analysis_note: The Iran-US ceasefire is collapsing: Iran attacked a cargo vessel June 26, the US responded with strikes on Iranian soil the same day, Israel continues escalating in Lebanon despite Washington pressure, and Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment with 1-2 week breakout window—creating a three-front escalation (maritime, air, nuclear) with diplomatic off-ramps narrowing.
